# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when .beads/ detected

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

○ long-running-harness-73v4 [EPIC] · W4: Bounce/Export & Automation UI   [● P1 · OPEN]
Owner: Nathan Delacrétaz · Type: epic
Created: 2026-03-29 · Updated: 2026-03-29

DESCRIPTION
Add the missing export workflow and automation lane editing.

SCOPE:
1. Bounce/Export dialog: add an 'EXPORT' button to the toolbar (TransportBar.tsx). On click, open a modal dialog with: range selection (Full Session / Loop Region radio), format display (WAV, 48kHz, 32-bit), a 'Bounce' button. On bounce: use BounceEngine from src/audio/bounce/ to render via chunked OfflineAudioContext. Show progress bar during rendering. On completion, trigger browser download of the WAV file. Add cancel button that terminates the bounce worker.
2. Automation Lane UI: create a minimal AutomationLaneEditor component that renders automation breakpoint curves on the arrangement canvas. The automation store (src/state/automation/) already has lane/point data and curve evaluation. The renderer needs to: draw the curve as connected line segments over the track lane, allow click-to-add-point, drag-to-move-point. Add a toggle per track in the arrangement header to show/hide automation lane.
3. Connect automation scheduling: verify that AutomationScheduler (src/audio/automation/) actually applies automation values to AudioParams during playback. The scheduler and ParamResolver exist -- verify the bridge from store to audio engine works end-to-end.

EARS: W-EVT-07.
DEPENDS ON: W1 (need tracks), W2 (need effects for automation targets).

KEY FILES TO MODIFY:
- src/ui/transport/TransportBar.tsx (add Export button)
- New: src/ui/session/BounceDialog.tsx (bounce modal)
- src/ui/arrangement/arrangement-renderer.ts (draw automation curves)
- New: src/ui/arrangement/AutomationLaneEditor.tsx (point editing)
- src/audio/bounce/ (verify BounceEngine integration)

QA: Create tracks with content, click Export, verify WAV downloads and sounds correct. Add automation lane to a fader, draw curve, play, verify parameter changes during playback.

DEPENDS ON
  → ✓ long-running-harness-7pq7: W1: Track Creation & Panel Wiring ● P0
  → ✓ long-running-harness-bs9b: W2: Effects, Routing & Mixer Integration ● P0

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the codebase. Let me create a detailed report:

## Comprehensive DAW Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the long-running-harness DAW project. Here's the complete picture for implementing bounce/export and automation UI features:

---

### **PROJECT SETUP**

**Test Runner:** Vitest with jsdom environment
- **Config:** `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/vitest.config.ts`
- Setup file: `/Users/Nathan/Documents/Code/long-running-harness/src/test/setup.ts`
- Coverage: v8 provider
- **Scripts:** `npm run test`, `npm run test:watch`, `npm run test:coverage`

**TypeScript Configuration:**
- **tsconfig.app.json:** Strict mode, ES2022 target, no emit
- **Path aliases:**
  - `@/*` → `src/*`
  - `@audio/*` → `src/audio/*`
  - `@ui/*` → `src/ui/*`
  - `@state/*` → `src/state/*`

**Dependencies:**
- React 19.2.4, React DOM 19.2.4
- Zustand 5.0.12 (state management)
- Zod 4.3.6 (schema validation)
- Vitest 4.1.1, @testing-library/react 16.3.2

---

### **1. BOUNCE/EXPORT SYSTEM**

#### **src/audio/bounce/** - Complete Bounce Engine

**Files:**
- `bounce-engine.ts` (683 lines) - Main bounce implementation
- `types.ts` - Type definitions
- `wav-encoder.ts` - WAV file encoding (16/24/32-bit)
- `synth-renderer.ts` - MIDI offline synth rendering
- `index.ts` - Public exports
- Tests: `bounce-engine.test.ts`, `wav-encoder.test.ts`, `synth-renderer.test.ts`

**Key Exports:**
```typescript
export { createBounceEngine } from "./bounce-engine";
export type {
  BounceEngine,
  BounceProgress,
  BounceResult,
  BounceOptions,
  BounceRange,
  TrackInstrumentConfig,
} from "./types";
export { encodeWav } from "./wav-encoder";
```

**BounceEngine Interface:**
- **Constructor:** `createBounceEngine(contextFactory?)` returns `BounceEngine`
- **Methods:**
  - `bounce(options: BounceOptions): AsyncGenerator<BounceProgress, BounceResult>` - async generator with progress yields
  - `cancel(): void` - cooperative cancellation flag

**Bounce Process Flow:**
1. **Phase: Preparing** (0-10%) - Session bounds computation, timeline building
2. **Phase: Rendering** (10-70%) - OfflineAudioContext rendering with full audio graph:
   - Mixer channel strips (gain, pan, mute per track)
   - Master bus with brickwall limiter (threshold -1dB, ratio 20:1, knee 0)
   - Audio clip scheduling with fade in/out and gain
   - MIDI clip pre-rendering (offline synth DSP)
   - Drum machine triggers (velocity-scaled, exponential decay)
   - Automation curve scheduling (volume/pan only)
3. **Phase: Encoding** (70-100%) - Chunked WAV encoding (30-second chunks for memory efficiency)

**Key Internal Functions (exported for testing):**
- `computeSessionBounds(tracks, clips)` - respects mute/solo state
- `buildEventTimeline(tracks, clips)` - separates audio vs MIDI clips
- `getAudibleTrackIds(tracks)` - applies mute/solo logic

**Automation Handling in Bounce:**
- Only mixer parameter lanes applied (`volume`, `pan`)
